title: Head-to-head: 3D Gaussian Splatting vs Instant-NGP
stumbles: wiki_page call with both node_id AND a `name` argument naming 'Nerfacto' returned 'node not found'; the agent recognised the mistake (mixed call shape + Nerfacto is not a separate corpus node) and proceeded with the data already gathered.
summary: Compared 3DGS and Instant-NGP head-to-head on Mip-NeRF 360 outdoor average. Initial matrix listed PSNR/SSIM/FPS/train time but no VRAM column; the researcher asked for training memory, and the agent pulled per-scene primitive counts and peak VRAM facts to land on a concrete 24 GB consumer-card recommendation: Instant-NGP fits any scene at ~3 GB peak, 3DGS-vanilla sits at the OOM edge on the largest Mip-NeRF 360 outdoor scenes (~22 GB peak on garden), and the Bulo revised-densification config gives a comfortable margin on all scenes.
